vulnerability.title: Disable Compression Or Set Compression to delayed
vulnerability.description: Compression is useful for slow network connections over long distances but can cause performance issues on local LANs. If use of compression is required, it should be enabled only after a user has authenticated; otherwise, it should be disabled. To disable compression or delay compression until after a user has successfully authenticated, add or correct the following line in the /etc/ssh/sshd_config file: Compression
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then var_sshd_disable_compression='' mkdir -p /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d touch /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*Compression\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config" LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*Compression\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"/*.conf if [ -e "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" ] ; then LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*Compression\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" else touch "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" fi # make sure file has newline at the end sed -i -e '$a\' "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" cp "/

triage_decision.reason: This is a standard SSH hardening measure to disable or delay compression, which aligns with STIG patterns for auth/ssh changes. It is unlikely to cause lockout or system breakage when applied correctly.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; networking affected; filesystems affected; Potential performance impact for users on slow network connections if compression is disabled

commands_executed: cat /etc/ssh/sshd_config 2>/dev/null | grep -i "compression" || echo "No compression line found"
[ -f /etc/ssh/sshd_config ] && grep -i "compression" /etc/ssh/sshd_config || echo "File not found or no compression line"

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
The finding is about disabling compression in SSHD configuration. According to the rule, I need to modify the /etc/ssh/sshd_config file to set Compression to "no" or "delayed". The recommendation suggests either adding a line or correcting an existing one.

Let me first read the current /etc/ssh/sshd_config to see if there's already a Compression line, and then decide what to do.

I'll:
1. Read /etc/ssh/sshd_config
2. Check for existing Compression configuration
3. If it exists, modify it to set Compression no (or delayed if required, but typically "no" is the secure setting)
4. If it doesn't exist, add it

The remediation script in the finding suggests:
- Remove any existing Compression lines
- Add a new line with Compression set to no (or delayed)

I'll follow the standard approach: read the file first, then use sed to modify it appropriately.
reasoning_messages.content: I'll help you remediate the OpenSCAP finding for disabling SSH compression. Let me start by examining the current SSHD configuration.
